commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer-labeled draft PR with compatibility-sensitive runtime behavior should receive human maintainer review; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff changes tool execution availability by disabling malformed schemas and does not add dependencies, secrets handling, or external code execution. (sha=b39a16ccf1201176f749f08277faf48d77ab3b1f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens Codex dynamic-tool registration and related MCP/provider schema normalization so malformed plugin tool schemas are quarantined or cleaned instead of breaking tool registration.

PR surface: Source +284, Tests +502, Generated 0. Total +786 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. there is a source-level reproduction path: current main projects only top-level schema constraints, while the PR tests show nested malformed schema nodes such as `properties.angle: null` reaching Codex dynamic-tool registration. I did not run the failing path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer-labeled draft PR with compatibility-sensitive runtime behavior should receive human maintainer review; there is no narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: fix(codex): quarantine unsupported dynamic tool schemas
target.body:
## Summary

- Harden Codex dynamic-tool registration so malformed runtime tool schemas are diagnosed and quarantined instead of failing assistant turn startup.
- Validate nested JSON Schema shape hazards that Codex rejects, including malformed schema maps, schema arrays, optional schema-valued keywords, `required`, property dependencies, and dynamic schema keywords.
- Preserve valid current-turn executable tools when only stale registered metadata for the same name is invalid.
- Inline local `$ref` schemas under MCP/tool-schema dependency and additional-item keywords so `$defs` are not stripped while dangling refs remain.
- Keep MCP loopback `tools/list` alive when a tool schema union contains malformed property-schema variants; malformed variants are ignored while later valid variants can still define the property.
- Preserve optionality when MCP loopback union schemas include boolean `true` branches instead of over-tightening merged `required` fields.
- Clean Gemini provider compatibility schemas inside schema-valued dependency and conditional branches so inspection and normalization agree.
- Clean shared provider compatibility schemas inside schema-valued dependency, conditional, tuple, and additional-item branches so xAI-style inspection and normalization agree.
- Keep test fixtures synthetic (`fuzzplugin`/`fuzz_move_*`) instead of naming the reported third-party plugin.
- Move plugin tool metadata storage into a lightweight module so schema diagnostics can name plugin/tool ownership without importing heavier plugin runtime code.
- Out of scope: changing global plugin materialization policy or rejecting valid non-Codex schemas outside the Codex/doctor projection path.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

No linked closing issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

No linked related issue.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Maintainer-directed hardening/debugging work.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: malformed runtime plugin tool schemas can make Codex reject dynamic tool registration before an assistant turn produces content.
- Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux runners, provider `aws`, using the branch over current `origin/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/plugins/tools.optional.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-tools.schema.test.ts src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-tools.materialize.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-core.config.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-gemini.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-xai.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts`
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-xai.test.ts`
  - `git fetch --no-tags --depth=1000 origin main:refs/remotes/origin/main && corepack pnpm check:changed`
  - `git diff --check`
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
- Evidence after fix:
  - AWS Crabbox `run_12ed1e8d221d` / `cbx_73dbc184e588`: 4 focused Vitest files, 110 tests passed.
  - AWS Crabbox `run_2f399ac63ad0` / `cbx_ed307000f628`: 5 focused Vitest files, 130 tests passed after the MCP/schema normalizer fix.
  - Local node-wrapper proof after final rebase: 5 focused Vitest files, 133 tests passed.
  - AWS Crabbox `run_a4c619c1328d` / `cbx_7f3c16fc4ad6`: final `corepack pnpm check:changed` passed over core, coreTests, extensions, extensionTests, and docs lanes on base `608fa52c80`.
  - Latest `origin/main` rebase sanity: rebased cleanly to `d88681662b`; touched-file set unchanged; `git diff --check` passed.
  - Local node-wrapper proof after MCP hardening: `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`, 63 tests passed across gateway projects.
  - AWS Crabbox `run_2e71a0b39626` / `cbx_7f0fdcd5b9eb`: final `corepack pnpm check:changed` passed over core, coreTests, extensions, extensionTests, and docs lanes on base `bf1a5c3303`.
  - Latest `origin/main` rebase sanity: rebased cleanly to `2e3b4b58a1`; touched schema/loopback file set unchanged; `git diff --check` passed.
  - Local explicit gateway-core proof after boolean-union review fix: `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`, 22 tests passed.
  - Local explicit unit-fast proof after Gemini dependency-branch hardening: 2 files, 28 tests passed.
  - AWS Crabbox `run_b5abe28468ea` / `cbx_56cf18977dc0`: explicit unit-fast proof passed 2 files, 28 tests on Linux Node 24.
  - Latest `origin/main` rebase sanity: rebased cleanly to `586a6ce03b`; focused local proof passed `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts` (22 tests) and Gemini/provider tests (28 tests).
  - AWS Crabbox `run_8d0ac71f67d7` / `cbx_7f7e115e0022`: final `corepack pnpm check:changed` passed over core, coreTests, extensions, extensionTests, and docs lanes.
  - Latest `origin/main` rebase sanity: rebased cleanly to `96bd939995`; touched schema/provider/MCP files did not drift; `git diff --check` passed; focused local proof passed `src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts` (22 tests) and Gemini/provider tests (28 tests).
  - Latest `origin/main` rebase sanity: rebased cleanly to `0c867eef75`; plugin SDK generated baseline hash was regenerated after upstream metadata changes.
  - Local explicit unit-fast proof after xAI-style provider-profile hardening: 2 files, 19 tests passed.
  - Local synthetic-fixture scrub proof after latest rebase: 2 files, 29 tests passed.
  - Local explicit unit-fast proof after latest provider-profile rebase: 3 files, 37 tests passed.
  - Local explicit gateway-core proof after latest MCP rebase: 1 file, 22 tests passed.
  - Direct oxlint passed on the touched provider-profile files.
  - Branch autoreview passed clean again after the latest rebase and synthetic fixture cleanup.
  - AWS Crabbox `run_a782b27386f3` / `cbx_b1ad6250dccd`: `corepack pnpm check:changed` passed over core, coreTests, extensions, extensionTests, and docs lanes.
  - Latest `origin/main` rebase sanity: rebased cleanly to `9c2a6a8df5`; new main commits did not touch this branch's schema/provider/Codex files; `git diff --check` passed.
  - Branch autoreview passed clean: no accepted/actionable findings.
- Observed result after fix: invalid Codex dynamic tool schemas are excluded from registration and disabled at call time with a clear OpenClaw error; valid executable tools with stale invalid registered metadata remain available.
- What was not tested: live provider conversation with a real malformed third-party plugin.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable in this shell because the CLI was not authenticated, so broad proof used direct AWS Crabbox.
- Before evidence: local Codex app-server fuzzing reproduced Codex rejection for malformed nested schema nodes.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts`
- AWS Crabbox `run_12ed1e8d221d` / `cbx_73dbc184e588`: focused Vitest command above.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-tools.schema.test.ts`
- AWS Crabbox `run_2f399ac63ad0` / `cbx_ed307000f628`: focused Vitest command above.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-tools.schema.test.ts src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tools.test.ts`
- AWS Crabbox `run_a4c619c1328d` / `cbx_7f3c16fc4ad6`: `corepack pnpm check:changed` after explicit `origin/main` fetch.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-core.config.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
- AWS Crabbox `run_2e71a0b39626` / `cbx_7f0fdcd5b9eb`: `corepack pnpm check:changed` after explicit `origin/main` fetch.
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-gemini.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts`
- AWS Crabbox `run_b5abe28468ea` / `cbx_56cf18977dc0`: explicit unit-fast focused Vitest command above.
- AWS Crabbox `run_8d0ac71f67d7` / `cbx_7f7e115e0022`: `corepack pnpm check:changed` after explicit `origin/main` fetch.
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-xai.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts`
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-gemini.test.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-xai.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts`
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-core.config.ts src/gateway/mcp-http.test.ts`
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.ts src/agents/schema/clean-for-xai.test.ts`
- AWS Crabbox `run_a782b27386f3` / `cbx_b1ad6250dccd`: `corepack pnpm check:changed` after explicit `origin/main` fetch.
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Core tool-schema projection tests for malformed nested keywords, valid tuple `items`, valid property dependencies, and synthetic tool names.
- Tool-parameter schema tests for `$ref` in `dependencies` and `additionalItems`.
- Codex dynamic-tool tests for quarantined invalid executable schemas, disabled-call response, and stale registered-only diagnostics that must not hide a valid executable tool.
- Doctor core check fixtures use synthetic `fuzzplugin`/`fuzz_move_*` names.
- MCP loopback regression for malformed union property schemas using synthetic `fuzzplugin_move_angles`.
- MCP loopback regression for boolean union optionality using synthetic `fuzzplugin_optional_angles`.
- Gemini provider compatibility regression for unsupported keywords inside schema-valued dependency branches using synthetic `fuzzplugin_move_angles`.
- xAI-style provider compatibility regression for unsupported keywords inside schema-valued dependency branches using synthetic schema/tool names.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Codex dynamic-tool registration could reject malformed active tool schemas before content was produced.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed Codex dynamic tools are now disabled instead of being advertised or crashing registration.

What is the highest-risk area?

Overly strict schema-shape validation could disable a valid runtime tool schema.

How is that risk mitigated?

The projection preserves valid JSON Schema forms covered by review, including boolean schemas, tuple `items`, `dependencies` string arrays, and schema-valued `additionalItems`; focused tests and changed-gate proof cover the touched surfaces.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Review the draft PR and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI has not been rechecked yet after the latest force-push; AWS Crabbox changed-gate proof passed before the final clean rebase, and the final rebase touched no schema/provider/Codex files.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local branch autoreview findings were addressed before opening: property dependencies, `additionalItems`, current executable tool preservation, tuple `items`, null schema keyword diagnostics, metadata import weight, and Codex-specific projection scoping.
